_documentation.description: PharmaGuide scoring configuration — modular category bonus architecture
_documentation.total_points: 80 calculated server-side (A+B+C+D), 20 fit score computed on device
_documentation.author: PharmaGuide Team
changelog: 3.4.0: Section C evidence scoring overhaul — Top-N diminishing returns aggregation (configurable weights, default [1.0, 0.5, 0.25]) to prevent multivitamin inflation. Added effect_direction multipliers (positive_strong/positive_weak/mixed/null/negative) to distinguish well-studied-effective from well-studied-ineffective.

3.3.0: Moved Section E (EPA/DHA dose adequacy) into Ingredient Quality as omega3_dose_bonus category bonus. Added category_bonus_pool.max_contribution=5 cap to prevent bonus stacking. Semantic naming convention: compute_*_score(), compute_*_bonus(), compute_*_penalty().

3.2.0: Added Section E dose adequacy (EPA+DHA, max 2.0 pts). Bands anchored to EFSA AI 250 mg, FDA QHC 500 mg, AHA CVD 1 g, EFSA TG claim 2 g, AHA/ACC Rx 4 g. PRESCRIPTION_DOSE_OMEGA3 flag at ≥4000 mg/day. A1/A2 parent-total dedup via is_parent_total field.
3.1.1: B1 cap raised from 5 to 8, B1 additive_id deduplication, all subsection caps now config-driven
3.1.0: Rebalanced section caps (B30/C20), A1 max 15, A6 single-efficiency bonus, B5 hidden-mass model, D reachability update
3.0.1: Normalized config to v3.0 section caps and penalty-sign convention; scoring gate kept open for rollout
3.0.0: Arithmetic-only scorer; matching and NLP moved to enrichment

_important_rules.config_driven: Point values and gates live here; scorer reads enriched fields and performs arithmetic only
_important_rules.penalty_sign_convention: Penalties are positive magnitudes and are subtracted in formulas
_important_rules.single_verdict: Verdict precedence is deterministic; first matching rule wins

feature_gates._require_full_mapping_note: ENFORCED: any unmapped active returns NOT_SCORED; keep blend/header classification clean.
feature_gates._probiotic_extended_scoring_note: When true, use extended probiotic bonus module (max +10)
feature_gates._allow_non_probiotic_probiotic_bonus_wit: When true, non-probiotic products can receive probiotic bonus only if strict evidence gates pass.
feature_gates._enable_d1_middle_tier_note: When true, manufacturers with verifiable mid-tier evidence (NSF GMP, FDA registered, USP, named GMP cert) receive D1_mid_tier_reputation points. Enricher computes eligibility via _has_verifiable_mid_tier_manufacturer_evidence.

paths._input_help: Directory containing enriched JSON files (relative to scripts/)
paths._output_help: Directory where scored output will be written

section_maximums._comment: v3.3 section caps. A+B+C+D = 80. Omega-3 dose adequacy is now a category bonus inside A (capped by category_bonus_pool.max_contribution). User profile (20 pts) is client-side only.
section_maximums._E_dose_adequacy_deprecated: Moved to section_A_ingredient_quality.omega3_dose_bonus in v3.3. Kept for backward compat during shadow comparison.
section_maximums._E_user_profile_note: Section E user-profile (Fit Score) is client-side only, not computed by this script
section_maximums._total_note: Quality score stored server-side. Combined score is Quality(80) + Fit(20) = 100 display

A1_bioavailability_form._max_rationale: Matches enricher's 0-18 raw bio_score range (premium form +15 plus natural +3). Previously clamped at 15 which threw away the natural-form bonus earned upstream.

A2_premium_forms._max_rationale: Raised from 3 to 5 to reward products that stack 4+ premium forms (e.g. chelated Mg + methylated B12 + K2-MK7 + D3 cholecalciferol). Previous cap compressed differentiation between good and best.

A3_delivery_system._note: Scoring reads enriched delivery_tier; no text matching at scoring time

synergy_cluster._description: Tiered synergy bonus based on evidence quality. Only ingredients with 2+ matching cluster members AND adequate doses qualify.

enzyme_recognition._description: Sprint E1.3.4 — small recognition credit for enzyme-containing products whose individual enzyme doses are labelled NP. Prevents misleading Section A = 0 on enzyme-dominated formulas while staying conservative. Deduplicated by canonical enzyme name; capped at max_points.
enzyme_recognition._philosophy: We reward verifiable enzyme activity, not just ingredient names. Activity-unit gate is disabled today (no upstream audit data yet) but can be flipped on once Dr Pham verifies DU/HUT/FIP values per product.

category_bonus_pool._description: Maximum total points from all category bonuses (probiotic, omega-3 dose, future). Core quality always dominates; bonuses enhance, not define.
category_bonus_pool._rationale: Without this cap, stacking niche bonuses could game Ingredient Quality. Core components (bioavailability, forms, delivery) must be the primary drivers.

probiotic_bonus._caps_note: Probiotic cap is mode-dependent: default_max=3 applies to general supplements (capped by category_bonus_pool.max_contribution=5 when combined with omega3). extended_max=10 applies only when probiotic_extended_scoring gate fires (dedicated probiotic products passing strict strain/CFU requirements). There is no single 'max' key by design — the effective cap varies by gate.
probiotic_bonus._prebiotic_terms_note: Substring terms used to detect prebiotic ingredients by name for the probiotic bonus prebiotic sub-score. Extend here without code changes.

probiotic_cfu_adequacy._description: Sprint E1.3.2.c — per-strain CFU adequacy uplift on top of probiotic_bonus. Points follow confidence: only single-strain blends with knowable per-strain CFU receive points; multi-strain blends and tier=None always contribute 0. Caps reflect clinical_support_level so weak evidence is constrained at 50% of tier points.
probiotic_cfu_adequacy._philosophy: Points follow confidence — not just presence. Never infer per-member CFU from blend totals.
probiotic_cfu_adequacy._per_product_max_rationale: Hard upper bound on the summed per-strain contribution even for single-strain-dominant multi-probiotic products. Keeps the uplift gradual across the catalog.

fish_oil_parent_mass_fallback._description: Sprint E1.3.3 — when EPA and DHA are individually labelled NP but the parent fish-oil / krill-oil row carries a total mass (propagated via E1.2.1), infer a conservative EPA+DHA estimate = parent_mass_mg * fraction. Flags omega3_dose_source='inferred_from_parent_mass' on the dose dict for transparency.
fish_oil_parent_mass_fallback._default_fraction_rationale: 0.5 is the midpoint between standard-grade fish oil (~30% EPA+DHA) and concentrated forms (~80%). Conservative enough to avoid overclaim on low-grade oils.

omega3_dose_bonus._max_rationale: Raised from 2.0 to 3.0 to restore the pre-merge value when EPA/DHA dose adequacy was standalone Section E. Still capped by category_bonus_pool.max_contribution=5 when stacked with probiotic. Bands redistributed so top tiers actually hit 3.0.
omega3_dose_bonus._description: EPA+DHA daily dose adequacy bonus (omega-3 products only). Applied only when explicit per-unit EPA and/or DHA amounts are labelled. Formerly standalone Section E, now a category bonus within Ingredient Quality.
omega3_dose_bonus._applicable: Products where ingredient_quality_data contains ingredients with canonical_id 'epa', 'dha', or 'epa_dha' and a usable quantity field.
omega3_dose_bonus._band_lookup: Uses per_day_mid = (min_serving/day + max_serving/day) / 2 x EPA+DHA mg per unit. Highest matching threshold wins.

_band_sources.250: EFSA (2012) AI for EPA+DHA: 250 mg/day general population
_band_sources.500: FDA qualified health claim minimum for combined EPA+DHA
_band_sources.1000: AHA recommendation for CVD patients: ~1 g/day EPA+DHA
_band_sources.2000: EFSA approved health claim for blood triglycerides: >= 2 g/day

section_B_safety_purity._formula: B = clamp(0, 30, base_score + min(bonus_pool_cap, bonuses) - penalties)

B0_immediate_fail._description: B0 safety-gate tier penalties. v5.0 status values drive the magnitudes below; pre-5.0 severity values fall back to the legacy `moderate_penalty` default.

B1_harmful_additives._cap_rationale: Raised from 8 to 15 so products stacking 5+ critical additives (3.0 each = 15) take the full penalty instead of being compressed to 8. Section B still clamps at 0 if the total drags negative, so there's no runaway damage.

B1_dietary_sugar_penalty._description: Amount-based sugar penalty layered on top of B1 named-sweetener penalty. Reads dietary_sensitivity_data.sugar.level from enricher output. Enables later personalization (e.g. stronger penalty for diabetic profiles) without code change.

B8_caers_adverse_events._description: DISABLED 2026-04-30: raw CAERS report counts are confounded by exposure base-rate (e.g. calcium has 2,145 serious reports because it is in every multivitamin, not because it is dangerous). Strong tier penalized RDA staples (calcium, vit D, fiber, fish oil, magnesium, protein) the same as kratom. Genuinely dangerous CAERS ingredients (kratom, ephedra, yohimbe, garcinia, DMAA) are already covered by B0 banned_recalled and B1 harmful_additives. Re-enable only with a PRR/ROR-normalized dataset or a curated causally-attributable allowlist.

section_C_evidence_research._top_n_weights_doc: Diminishing returns: best ingredient 100%, 2nd 70%, 3rd 50%, 4th 30%. Softer than original [1.0,0.5,0.25] — rewards targeted formulations with 4 evidence-backed actives without inflating multivitamins (5th+ still 0).
section_C_evidence_research._effect_direction_doc: Scales evidence score by whether studies show benefit. Entries without effect_direction default to positive_strong (1.0x) for backward compatibility.
section_C_evidence_research._enrollment_quality_doc: Bands: [threshold, multiplier]. <50 -> 0.6x, 50-199 -> 0.8x, 200-499 -> 1.0x, 500-999 -> 1.1x, >=1000 -> 1.2x (default). Only applies to RCTs and meta-analyses, not observational or preclinical.
section_C_evidence_research._depth_bonus_doc: Discrete bands: [threshold, bonus]. 0-19 trials +0.0, 20-39 +0.25, 40+ +0.5. Uses published_studies count from matched entry. Added after top-N aggregation, before section cap.

section_D_brand_trust._D1_manufacturer_reputation_note: Awarded when product matches a trusted manufacturer (exact match or is_trusted_manufacturer flag).
section_D_brand_trust._D1_mid_tier_reputation_note: Awarded when product has verifiable mid-tier evidence (NSF GMP / FDA registered / USP / named GMP cert). Gated by feature_gates.enable_d1_middle_tier.

D4_high_standard_region._description: Points awarded when the manufacturing region is in the high-standard regulatory set below. Legacy scalar form (e.g. just `1.0`) still works and falls back to the default 12-country list baked into the scorer.

section_E_dose_adequacy._max_rationale: v3.4.x: synced from section_A_ingredient_quality.omega3_dose_bonus.max. Legacy compat block kept so consumers reading section_scores['E_dose_adequacy'] see the same value as the canonical omega3_dose_bonus.
section_E_dose_adequacy._description: EPA+DHA daily dose adequacy bonus (omega-3 products only). Applied only when explicit per-unit EPA and/or DHA amounts are labelled. Score is an additive bonus within the overall 80-pt quality ceiling.
section_E_dose_adequacy._applicable: Products where ingredient_quality_data contains ingredients with canonical_id 'epa', 'dha', or 'epa_dha' and a usable quantity field.
section_E_dose_adequacy._band_lookup: Uses per_day_mid = (min_serving/day + max_serving/day) / 2 × EPA+DHA mg per unit. Highest matching threshold wins.

_band_sources.250: EFSA (2012) AI for EPA+DHA: 250 mg/day general population
_band_sources.500: FDA qualified health claim minimum for combined EPA+DHA
_band_sources.1000: AHA recommendation for CVD patients: ~1 g/day EPA+DHA
_band_sources.2000: EFSA approved health claim for blood triglycerides: ≥2 g/day
_band_sources.4000: AHA/ACC prescription dose for hypertriglyceridemia (Lovaza/Vascepa); AF risk threshold confirmed in REDUCE-IT, STRENGTH, ASCEND, VITAL trials

manufacturer_violations._note: Read total_deduction_applied and add directly to quality_raw

verdict_logic._poor_threshold_quality_score_note: Quality score (0-80 scale) below this threshold yields verdict=POOR; at/above yields SAFE. Keep in sync with grade_scale.Low.min.
